findings.description: Header typography and emphasis are not fully preserved.
findings.evidence: On page 1, the PDF title block uses centered serif text with the date and time italicized/bolded in a compact stacked layout. The HTML renders the same content in a different sans-serif style with different emphasis and a horizontal rule, reducing fidelity of heading formatting though meaning remains clear.

findings.description: Section heading alignment/style differs from the source.
findings.evidence: On page 1, PDF section labels such as 'Call to Order and Introductions' with presenter names appear in a two-part line with wider spacing; the HTML combines them with an em dash and different font weight/size. Content is present but formatting is simplified.

findings.description: Section heading styling differs from the source formatting.
findings.evidence: On page 2, headings such as 'AT Expo – October 5th, 2023', 'Stephen E Sallee Committee', and 'Announcements/updates' appear in the PDF with separated left/right alignment and serif styling, while the HTML renders them inline with em dashes and sans-serif styling. Structure remains understandable.

findings.description: Several hyperlinks/linked phrases from the PDF were not faithfully preserved as actual URLs or equivalent explicit link text in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF includes explicit linked references such as "Meeting Registration - Zoom (zoomgov.com)", "Walk & Roll Together Gaston - DRNC (disabilityrightsnc.org)", "Assistive Technology Loans | Self-Help Credit Union", and "NCATP Grant Advisory Council Stephen E Sallee (office.com)". HTML preserves these only as plain text labels without the corresponding explicit destination domains/URLs in some cases, reducing link fidelity.

findings.description: A few small text inaccuracies/normalizations are present, though they do not materially change meaning.
findings.evidence: PDF attendee list shows "Tara Muller" while body text under member updates appears as "Tara Mueller shared..."; HTML uses "Tara Mueller shared...". PDF heading line reads "Workforce Solution" and HTML uses the same, but body contains minor wording/grammar normalization such as "also have apprenticeships available" retained, while other punctuation and apostrophe forms are normalized (e.g., curly quotes/HTML entities).
